<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible (prioritizing Y, then X) </my goals>
<reason> This is the final round of trading, so my objective is to maximize Y acquisition while minimizing the loss of X, given my current holdings (25X, 5Y). My strategy leverages my surplus of X to extract as much Y as possible from Player BLUE.

1. **Optimal opening offer**:
   I propose trading all 25X for 15Y. This aggressive but balanced offer tests Player BLUE’s valuation of Y while maximizing my potential gain. Since Y is my scarcity and X my surplus, this trade positions me to acquire critical resources without overcommitting.

2. **Risk management**:
   If Player BLUE rejects, they must either propose a better deal (e.g., more Y for less X) or leave me to accept their counter. Since this is the last round, any Y gained is a net positive, so I will accept any offer with >0Y if no better alternative exists.
   If Player BLUE counters with a worse deal (e.g., 25X for <15Y), I will reject and wait for a better offer—or accept if their proposal is the only available option.

3. **Fallback planning**:
   If Player BLUE proposes nothing (NONE), I will accept my original offer (25X for 15Y) to force a resolution, ensuring at least some Y is acquired.
   My message emphasizes urgency ("final trade") and fairness to encourage acceptance while signaling my willingness to negotiate further if needed.

This approach maximizes my potential gain while accounting for Player BLUE’s unknown inventory, ensuring I exit the game with the best possible outcome. </reason>
